description: African fintech regulatory jurisdiction data — sourced from central bank circulars, data protection acts, and FATF mutual evaluation reports

zimbabwe.notes: ZWCMP compliance for mobile money. RBZ National Payment Systems regulations.

south_africa.cross_border_conditions: Consent or adequate jurisdiction (POPIA §72). Binding corporate rules accepted.
south_africa.notes: FSCA regulates markets. FIC handles AML. Most mature regulatory framework on the continent.

nigeria.cross_border_conditions: Adequacy assessment or NDPC approval. CBN requires African data residency for financial data.
nigeria.notes: Largest fintech market in Africa. CBN licensing framework for PSPs, MMOs, and switching companies.

egypt.cross_border_conditions: DPC approval required. Financial data must remain accessible to CBE.
egypt.notes: me-south-1 (Bahrain) is closest. FRA regulates non-banking financial services.

kenya.cross_border_conditions: Adequate safeguards proven to ODPC. Data sovereignty not strictly required but CBK preference.
kenya.notes: M-Pesa market. CBK Payment Service Providers Regulations 2014. CMA regulates securities.

ghana.cross_border_conditions: DPC registration required. BoG allows EU-hosted infrastructure for financial data.
ghana.notes: BoG Payment Systems and Services Act 2019. E-money issuer licensing. eu-west-1 permitted.

tanzania.cross_border_conditions: No formal restrictions yet. BoT prefers regional data residency.
tanzania.notes: National Payment Systems Act 2015. TCRA licenses mobile money. Data protection law pending.

rwanda.cross_border_conditions: NCSA authorization required for transfers outside Rwanda.
rwanda.notes: Kigali International Financial Centre. BNR fintech sandbox. iRembo digital services platform.

waemu.regulator_full: Banque Centrale des États de l'Afrique de l'Ouest
waemu.cross_border_conditions: Within WAEMU: free movement. Outside: country-specific DPA rules apply.
waemu.notes: BCEAO Instruction 01/2017. OHADA Uniform Act for commercial records. CFA franc zone.

cemac.regulator_full: Banque des États de l'Afrique Centrale
cemac.cross_border_conditions: Within CEMAC: free movement. Outside: country-specific rules.
cemac.notes: COBAC regulations. OHADA Uniform Act. CFA franc (XAF) zone.

eac.cross_border_conditions: EAC Financial Integration Framework facilitates cross-border data flows.
eac.currency: Various (KES, TZS, UGX, RWF, BIF, SSP)
eac.notes: EAC integration creates regional compliance baseline. Individual country laws take precedence.
